name: Wichita Falls Teachers
description_short: Wichita Falls Teachers Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving educators and students in the Wichita Falls area since 1937, offering savings accounts, loans, credit cards, and retirement accounts.
description_long:
Wichita Falls Teachers Federal Credit Union was founded on April 8, 1937, in Room 122 of Wichita Falls High School as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative. Originally serving only Wichita Falls Board of Education employees with a $5.25 membership fee, the credit union has expanded to serve a broader educational community over nearly nine decades. Today it operates as a federally-insured credit union under NCUA oversight, maintaining its roots in the education sector while growing its membership base.

WFTFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts (share accounts with $50 minimum, $10 for students), certificate accounts (6 months to 5 years, $1,000 minimum), Individual Retirement Accounts (both Roth and Traditional with no minimum balance), checking accounts, credit cards (4.9% APR promotional rate for 12 months), and personal and auto loans. Members can access online and mobile banking, direct deposit services, and ATM access through the 5,000-credit-union Shared Branch network. The credit union also offers UChoose Rewards, where members earn 1 point per $2 spent on purchases, redeemable for gift cards, vacations, and other rewards.

WFTFCU distinguishes itself through its focus on the educational community and commitment to member education. The institution provides financial education resources, security and fraud prevention information, and community involvement opportunities beyond standard banking services. Their membership eligibility is clearly defined—open to employees and students of Wichita Falls ISD and surrounding school districts, Midwestern State University students, and Vernon College employees and students. This targeted membership model allows the credit union to tailor services to educators' and students' specific financial needs.

The credit union offers genuine convenience with extended drive-through hours (8:30am-6pm weekdays, 9am-Noon Saturday), access to 5,000 shared branch locations, and robust digital banking options. However, potential members should note that membership is restricted to specific educational institutions and their communities—it is not open to the general public. Additionally, while the promotional credit card rate is competitive, the website does not disclose ongoing APR rates or rates for other loan products, making full cost comparison difficult.
services: Share savings accounts with $50 minimum deposit
Savings Stackers youth accounts for children ages 12 and under
Certificate accounts (6 months to 5 years, $1,000 minimum)
Mini-Jumbo Certificates ($50,000 minimum, 6 months to 2 years)
Individual Retirement Accounts (Traditional and Roth IRAs)
Credit cards with promotional 4.9% APR for 12 months
UChoose Rewards program with point accumulation and redemption
pros: Access to 5,000 shared branch ATM locations nationwide through Shared Branch network
Extended drive-through hours until 6pm weekdays and Saturday morning service
4.9% APR credit card promotional rate for 12 months
No minimum balance requirement for Individual Retirement Accounts (Roth and Traditional)
UChoose Rewards program offering 1 point per $2 spent with redeemable prizes
Targeted financial education and community involvement programs for members
Very low savings account minimums ($50 standard, $10 for students, $10 for Savings Stackers)
Member-owned structure means profits return to members rather than shareholders
cons: Membership is restricted to employees/students of specific educational institutions—not open to general public
Website does not disclose standard credit card APR rates after promotional period ends
Limited location details—single physical branch location in Wichita Falls limits in-person service accessibility
Certificate account early withdrawal penalties are mentioned but not specified on the website
No information provided about rates for personal loans, auto loans, or other loan products beyond promotional credit card rate
best_for: Teachers and school administrators in the Wichita Falls ISD and surrounding school districts
Midwestern State University and Vernon College students seeking accessible financial services
Educators seeking member-owned financial institution with community focus and financial education resources
Families wanting to teach children about saving through the Savings Stackers youth program
diagnosis: Wichita Falls Teachers FCU is best for educators, school staff, and students affiliated with Wichita Falls ISD, surrounding school districts, Midwestern State University, or Vernon College who want a member-owned credit union with educational focus and competitive rates. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to these specific educational communities and is not available to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
